     The Numbers Business Report

12 Strong (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $45,819,713Details
International Box Office $25,298,665
Worldwide Box Office $71,118,378
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,770,936
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,122,310
Total $10,893,246
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In the harrowing days following 9/11, a U.S. Special Forces team, led by their new Captain, Mitch Nelson, is chosen to be the first U.S. troops sent into Afghanistan for an extremely dangerous mission. There, in the rugged mountains, they must convince Northern Alliance General Dostum to join forces with them to fight their common adversary: the Taliban and their Al Qaeda allies. In addition to overcoming mutual distrust and a vast cultural divide, the Americans—accustomed to state-of-the-art warfare—must adopt the rudimentary tactics of the Afghan horse soldiers. But despite their uneasy bond, the new allies face overwhelming odds: outnumbered and outgunned by a ruthless enemy that does not take prisoners.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$15,815,025 (34.5% of total gross)
Legs:2.90 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:64.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,002 opening theaters/3,018 max. theaters, 4.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $60,154,088

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 19th, 2018 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
January 19th, 2018 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: January 16th, 2018 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
January 17th, 2018 (Wide) (Philippines)
January 17th, 2018 (Wide) (Egypt)
January 18th, 2018 (Wide) (Thailand)
January 18th, 2018 (Wide) (Greece)
Video Release: April 10th, 2018 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for war violence and language throughout.
(Rating bulletin 2502 (Cert #51285), 11/15/2017)
Running Time: 90 minutes
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Afghanistan
Plot point: 9/11, C.I.A., Special Ops, Taliban
Social setting: Afghanistan War
Subgenre: War Drama
Time period setting: 2000s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Alcon Entertainment, Black Label Media, Jerry Bruckheimer Films, Torridon Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In the harrowing days following 9/11, a U.S. Special Forces team, led by their new Captain, Mitch Nelson, is chosen to be the first U.S. troops sent into Afghanistan for an extremely dangerous mission. There, in the rugged mountains, they must convince Northern Alliance General Dostum to join forces with them to fight their common adversary: the Taliban and their Al Qaeda allies. In addition to overcoming mutual distrust and a vast cultural divide, the Americans—accustomed to state-of-the-art warfare—must adopt the rudimentary tactics of the Afghan horse soldiers. But despite their uneasy bond, the new allies face overwhelming odds: outnumbered and outgunned by a ruthless enemy that does not take prisoners.
